page.title: Something went wrong
page.message: We hit an unexpected problem on this page. Try again, and if it keeps happening, let your administrator know.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.message: This page doesn't exist or may have been moved.

llm.out_of_credits: The assistant is paused: the AI account is out of credit. Ask your administrator to top it up.
llm.invalid_key: The assistant isn't connected right now. Your administrator needs to check the AI provider settings.
llm.rate_limited: The assistant is busy right now. Please try again in a few moments.
llm.model_unavailable: The selected AI model isn't available right now. Try a different model, or ask your administrator.
llm.context_too_long: This conversation got too long for the model. Start a new chat or shorten your message and try again.
llm.network: Couldn't reach the AI service. Please try again in a moment.
llm.timed_out: This task took too long and was stopped. Please try again, or break it into smaller steps.
llm.interrupted: The task was interrupted before it finished. Please try again.
llm.unknown: Something went wrong while generating a response. Please try again. If it keeps happening, let your administrator know.

providerStatus.outOfCredits: The AI account is out of credit — top it up to restore the assistant for everyone
providerStatus.invalidKey: The AI provider isn't connected — check the provider settings to restore the assistant

nav.startNewChat: Start a new chat to get going

panel.greetingHint: Drop a file here, or just describe what you need

extractText.label: Extract the text
extractText.prompt: Extract the text from the PDF I'll attach:

fillForm.label: Fill in a form
fillForm.prompt: Fill in the form fields in the PDF I'll attach:

toWord.label: Convert to Word
toWord.prompt: Convert the PDF I'll attach into an editable Word document.

analyze.label: Analyze the data
analyze.prompt: Analyze the spreadsheet I'll attach and summarize the key trends:

chart.label: Build a chart
chart.prompt: Build a clear chart from the spreadsheet I'll attach.

clean.label: Clean up the data
clean.prompt: Clean up and tidy the data in the spreadsheet I'll attach.

translate.label: Translate it
translate.prompt: Translate the document I'll attach into

reformat.label: Make it look clean
reformat.prompt: Reformat the document I'll attach to look clean and professional.

ocr.label: Pull out the text
ocr.prompt: Extract the text from the image I'll attach:

removeBg.label: Remove the background
removeBg.prompt: Remove the background from the image I'll attach.

resize.label: Resize or convert

panel.telegramReadOnly: This is a Telegram chat. Reply there.
panel.telegramBusy: The bot is working on this chat…
panel.modelGoneBody: This model isn't available anymore — pick another from the switcher above to keep chatting

menu.deleteDescription: This will permanently delete “{title}” and all its messages.

share.title: Share chat
share.description: Choose who can view this chat via the link.
share.linkHint: Viewable by everyone, even without an account
share.usersHint: Only people with an account here
share.gateTitle: This chat is for signed-in users only
share.gateDescription: Sign in to your account to view this conversation.

model.needKey: Enter your API key to load models
model.noneFoundHint: To use a model that isn't listed, type its full ID (e.g. openrouter/owl-alpha)
model.currentUnavailable: The current model is unavailable. Pick another below.
model.context: Context size: how much it can keep in mind at once

input.placeholder: Assign a task or ask anything
input.placeholderFiles: Add a message about the files…
input.awaitingInput: Respond to the request above to continue

ask.title: A quick question
ask.expired: That question is no longer active — your answer wasn't saved.

workspace.title: Files
workspace.createHint: Send a message to create the workspace.

preview.gone: This file is no longer in the chat's workspace
preview.temporary: This file is temporarily unavailable, try again in a moment

notice.blindModalities: This model can't view {modalities} directly — pick one that can:

message.genericError: Something went wrong while generating a response. Please try again.

archived.title: Archived chats
archived.confirmDescription: This action cannot be undone. The chat and all its messages will be permanently deleted.

hook.rateLimited: Too many messages, please wait a moment.
hook.budgetReached: Spending limit reached. Please contact your admin.

manage.expired: This confirmation expired — just ask again.
manage.applyError: Couldn't apply that — please try again.
manage.emptyConnectors: No connectors yet. Just ask — e.g. "connect Linear".
manage.signedIn: I've signed in — please check the connector now.
manage.pickFolderHint: Choose a folder on your computer to connect.

folders.tooLarge: This folder is too large to sync ({count} files, {mb} MB). Limit: {maxFiles} files / {maxMb} MB.
folders.unsupportedBrowser: Live folder sync needs Chrome or Edge.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Organize your chats with custom instructions and models.
projects.empty: No projects yet. Create one to organize your chats.
projects.deleteError: Could not delete project. Please try again.
projects.confirmDeleteDesc: Chats will be kept but unlinked from this project.
projects.createError: Could not create project. Please try again.
projects.updateError: Could not update project. Please try again.
projects.createDesc: Create a new project to organize your chats.

form.name: Name
form.defaultModelHint: Overrides the global default for chats in this project.
form.internetHint: Let the assistant download files and reach the internet while working on this project.

usersPage.title: Users
usersPage.subtitle: Manage user roles and permissions. Only admins can access this page.
usersPage.loadError: Could not load users. Please refresh the page.

general.name: Name
general.appearanceDesc: Choose how Capka looks on your device.
general.registrationDesc: Control whether new users can create accounts.
general.allowRegistrationHint: When disabled, only admins can add new users. Existing users can still log in.
general.shareProvidersHint: When enabled, users without their own provider key use the admin's key (and budget). Off by default.
general.author: Built by <author>@LyoSU</author>

memory.title: Agent Memory
memory.subtitle: What the AI remembers across conversations. It maintains these notes itself — you can edit them too.
memory.userDesc: Facts and preferences the AI carries into every chat.
memory.projectDesc: What the AI remembers about a specific project's work.
memory.placeholder: The AI fills this in as you chat. You can add or edit notes here too — one per line.
memory.loadError: Could not load memory. Please refresh the page.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.subtitle: Connect external services to Capka.

telegram.title: Telegram Bot
telegram.desc: Enter your Telegram bot token from @BotFather to enable Telegram integration.

link.title: Link Telegram Account
link.desc: Connect your Telegram account to chat with Capka from Telegram.
link.openBotHint: One tap and you're connected, no copying needed.
link.orManually: Not working? Send this to the bot by hand:
link.sendCommand: Send this command to the bot in Telegram:
link.configureFirstUser: Not set up yet. Ask your administrator to connect Telegram.
link.oidcFailed: Couldn't connect to Telegram. Please try again.

integrations.linkMovedHint: Users link their own Telegram accounts from Settings → General.

limits.title: Your limit
limits.hint: Shows how much of your shared-key budget you've used.

billing.title: Keys & limits
billing.subtitle: Control where provider keys come from and how much users may spend on the shared key.

mode.title: Provider key mode
mode.desc: Decides whether users run on the admin's shared key, their own, or both.

shared_plus_own.label: Shared + own
shared_plus_own.desc: The admin key is the default for everyone. Users may add their own and run on it.

shared_only.label: Shared only
shared_only.desc: Everyone uses the admin key. Users cannot add their own.

own_only.label: Own only
own_only.desc: No shared key, every user connects their own.

limits.title: Shared-key spending limits
limits.desc: Per-user budget across three windows. Leave a field empty for no limit. Own keys are never limited.
limits.hint: Amounts in $. When a window is exhausted, new requests are blocked while running ones finish.
limits.ownOnlyNote: In own-only mode there's no shared key, so limits don't apply.

tiers.title: User tiers
tiers.soon: Limits are the same for everyone for now (the default tier). Per-user tiers are coming later.

connections.title: LLM Providers
connections.subtitle: Manage AI provider connections and API keys.
connections.emptyHint: Add an API key to start using AI models
connections.toggleError: Could not change state. Please try again.
connections.shareWithUsersHint: This key joins the shared pool. Turn off to keep it private to you.
connections.confirmRemoveDesc: This will disconnect the provider and remove its API key.
connections.loadError: Could not load providers. Please refresh the page.
connections.activateError: Could not activate provider. Please try again.
connections.removeError: Could not remove provider. Please try again.
connections.modelUpdateError: Could not update default model. Please try again.
connections.saved: Provider saved and set as active
connections.saveError: Could not save provider settings. Please check your API key and try again.
connections.modelFilterDesc: Limits for users on your shared key. Your own access is unaffected.
connections.minContextHint: Models with context below this are hidden. Default: 100k.
connections.maxPriceHint: Pricier models are hidden and blocked on the shared key. 0 means no cap.
connections.maxContextTokensHint: How much of the context window a conversation may fill before it's compacted. 0 means the model's full window. Caps shared-key cost (e.g. 200000 even on a 1M-window model).
connections.resyncModelsHint: Refresh the model list and metadata (prices, context, capabilities) from OpenRouter. Runs automatically every 12h — use this to pull updates now.
connections.resyncFailed: Failed to re-sync the model catalog
connections.chatCompletionsHint: OpenAI uses its Responses API by default. Turn this on only if your setup needs the classic Chat Completions endpoint.

usage.title: Usage & cost
usage.subtitleShared: What the organization is spending on its shared AI key.
usage.subtitleOwn: How members are using their own provider keys — their own spend, not the organization's.

skills.title: Customize
skills.subtitle: Everything that extends what your assistant can do: skills, connectors, and plugins.

group.teamHint: Uploaded for everyone in the organization.

skills.emptyTitle: No skills of your own yet
skills.emptyHint: Skills are reusable instructions the assistant loads when they fit a task. Upload your own here — skills that come with plugins are managed on the Plugins tab.
skills.noDescriptionHint: No description. Add one so the assistant knows when to use this.
skills.deleteWarn: This removes the skill for everyone it's available to. This can't be undone.
skills.deletePluginWarn: This skill came from the “{plugin}” plugin and may return next time that plugin is installed or updated. To remove it for good, uninstall the plugin.

installed.empty: No plugins installed yet. Open Marketplace to add one.
installed.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall “{name}”? This removes all of its skills and connectors.
installed.memberInstallHint: When on, members can install plugins for themselves from your marketplaces. Off — only you (admin) install, org-wide.
installed.reviewConnectorsWarning: This update changes a connector definition — it can introduce code that runs in your sandbox. Review before applying.

skills.chatHint: You can also add and manage these just by asking the assistant in chat.

automations.title: Automations
automations.subtitle: Scheduled agent runs that fire on their own, without a chat open.
automations.chatHint: Ask the assistant in chat to set one up, e.g. “Every Monday at 9am, prepare the weekly summary.”
automations.emptyHint: An automation runs a saved instruction on a schedule or at a set time. Ask the assistant in chat to create one.
automations.overdueHint: This run is overdue — the background worker may not be running. Contact your admin if it doesn't recover.
automations.deleteWarn: This stops future runs and removes the automation. This can't be undone.

connectors.title: Connectors
connectors.subtitle: Connect external services (Notion, Linear, GitHub…) your assistant can use.

method.label: Authentication
method.noneHint: This service needs no access key — just save.
method.oauthHint: You'll sign in after saving: we'll open the service's page to confirm.

connectors.shareWithUsersHint: A shared connector for everyone. Turn off to keep it private.
connectors.tokenHint: If the service needs an access key, paste it here. It's stored encrypted.
connectors.advancedHint: OAuth Client ID / Secret are only for services that require a pre-registered client. Leave blank for the usual sign-in.
connectors.testOauth: This connector uses sign-in. Save it, then click Sign in.

health.unauthorized: Token rejected, check the access key

connectors.commandHint: Runs inside the sandbox. The package installs itself on first use (e.g. npx, uvx).
connectors.envHint: Environment variables (e.g. access keys), if the package needs any. Optional.

marketplace.title: Marketplace
marketplace.subtitle: Add a plugin marketplace, then install skills and connectors for your whole organization with one click.
marketplace.adminOnly: Only admins can manage the marketplace.
marketplace.empty: No marketplaces yet. Add one above to browse plugins.
marketplace.uninstallConfirm: Uninstall “{name}”? This removes all of its skills and connectors.
marketplace.unsupportedSource: This plugin's source isn't installable yet.
marketplace.tokenHint: Optional. Lets Capka reach marketplaces without hitting GitHub's anonymous rate limit — the usual cause of a failed install — and open private repositories. Stored encrypted; never shown again.
marketplace.tokenPlaceholder: Paste a token to set or replace

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.subtitle: Control what the assistant may use, for everyone in the organization.
permissions.empty: Nothing to govern yet. Add skills or connectors first.
permissions.hint: Deny hides a skill or connector from the assistant for everyone. Allow is the default.
permissions.askSoon: Ask (human-in-the-loop) is coming soon. For now it behaves as Allow.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.subtitle: How people sign in, and who is allowed to create an account.
authentication.actionFailed: Something went wrong. Please try again.

telegram.title: Login with Telegram
telegram.desc: Let people sign in with one tap using their Telegram account.
telegram.botFatherHint: In @BotFather open your bot → Bot Settings → Web Login. Add the redirect URI below to Allowed URLs, then copy the Client ID and Secret here.
telegram.redirectHint: Paste this into BotFather → Web Login → Allowed URLs.

mode.title: Who can register
mode.desc: What happens when someone first creates an account, via email or Telegram.

open.label: Open
open.desc: Anyone can sign in and start using the app right away.

approval.label: By approval
approval.desc: Anyone can sign in, but an admin must approve them before they get access.

closed.label: Closed
closed.desc: Only existing accounts can sign in. No new sign-ups.

email.title: Email sign-up
email.desc: Allow creating accounts with an email and password. Turn off to leave Telegram as the only way in. Existing email accounts keep signing in.
email.deadEndWarning: Email sign-up is off and Telegram login isn't configured — new users won't be able to register at all.

pending.title: Waiting for approval
pending.desc: People who signed in and need your go-ahead.
pending.empty: No one is waiting right now.

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Protect provider keys, isolate code execution, and restrict outbound connections.
security.adminOnly: Only admins can manage security settings.
security.encryptionKeyDesc: Protects the AI provider API keys stored in your database.

masterKey.insecureBody: The key that encrypts your AI provider keys is stored in the database. Anyone who can read the database can read those keys. Copy this line into your server's environment file, then restart Capka:
masterKey.secureLeftover: Protected by an environment key. An old copy is still in the database. Remove it to finish.
masterKey.secureClean: Provider keys are protected by an environment key.

security.sandboxDesc: Give AI full access to a sandboxed Linux environment with Python, Node.js, and dev tools.
security.enableSandboxHint: AI can execute commands, read/write files, and run code in an isolated container per chat.
security.networkDesc: Control how strictly provider connections are restricted.
security.blockPrivateHint: Prevents provider URLs (LiteLLM, Ollama) from pointing at private or loopback addresses. Leave off if you self-host a gateway on your local network. Cloud-metadata addresses are always blocked.
security.sandboxNetHint: Let sandboxed code and local (stdio) connectors reach the public internet. Tools that self-install on first use (e.g. npx) need this. Private/internal ranges and cloud metadata stay blocked.
security.netEnabled: Internet access on for the sandbox.
security.netDisabled: Internet access off for the sandbox.
security.sandboxNetBlocked: Blocked at the deployment level — set SANDBOX_ALLOW_NETWORK=true and restart the sandbox controller to enable this.
security.hostFoldersHint: Let admins attach a folder from the server (the machine running Capka) into a chat's sandbox at /folders/<name>, read-only by default. Off by default.
security.hostFoldersEnabled: Server folders on — admins can attach host folders.
security.pcFoldersHint: Let users connect a folder from their own computer (synced into the chat workspace via the browser). Chrome or Edge only for live sync.
security.agentDesc: How much the assistant may change on its own from chat.
security.autonomousModeHint: When on, the agent applies settings changes and installs skills directly, without a confirmation each time — it still asks before installing a connector that runs third-party code. When off, it asks you to confirm each risky change.
security.autonomousEnabled: Autonomous mode on — the agent acts without asking.
security.autonomousDisabled: Autonomous mode off — the agent confirms risky changes.

updates.title: Updates
updates.subtitle: Keep Capka up to date with the latest release.
updates.howToHint: Run this on the host where Capka is installed — your data and settings are kept.
updates.autoCheckHint: Periodically asks GitHub for the latest release. Turn off to make no outbound calls.

brand.headline: Calm access to AI for your whole team
brand.sub: Connect a provider once. After that everyone just opens a chat and works, in the browser or in Telegram.
brand.point1: One shared key for the whole company
brand.point2: You choose which models are available
brand.point3: The same assistant in the browser and Telegram

account.title: Create your account
account.subtitle: Set up your admin account to get started
account.name: Name
account.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
account.error: Could not create account. Please try again.
account.setupTokenHint: Open the setup link you were given when the server was installed and this fills in by itself — otherwise paste the setup token from that link, or ask whoever set up the server.

provider.title: Configure AI provider
provider.subtitle: Connect an AI provider to power your assistant
provider.testError: Could not verify the connection. Please check your details and try again.
provider.saveError: Could not save provider settings. Please try again.
provider.verifyError: Could not verify API key. Please check the key and try again.

telegram.title: Telegram integration
telegram.subtitle: Optionally connect a Telegram bot
telegram.hint: Get a token from @BotFather on Telegram. You can skip this and set it up later.
telegram.finishError: Could not complete setup. Please try again.

login.title: Sign in
login.description: Enter your credentials to access Capka.
login.invalidCredentials: Invalid email or password. Please try again.

register.title: Create account
register.description: Sign up to start using Capka.
register.disabledDescription: New account registration is currently disabled by the administrator.

telegram.failed: Telegram sign-in didn't go through. Please try again.
telegram.registerHint: Sign in with your Telegram account to get started.

errors.userExists: An account with this email already exists.
errors.emailNotVerified: Confirm your email before signing in.

pending.title: Almost there
pending.description: Your account was created and is waiting for approval.
pending.hint: An administrator needs to approve your account before you can start. You'll be able to sign in as soon as they do.

telegram.start:
👋 **Welcome to Capka!**

I'm your AI assistant right here in Telegram — I answer questions and work with text, photos and files.

To get started, open Capka and sign in with Telegram — your account will be linked automatically.

Already have a connection code? Send `/link CODE`.
telegram.startLinked:
👋 **Welcome back!** Your account is already connected.

Just message me and I'll get going. You can send text, photos and files, and /new opens a fresh chat.
telegram.tooManyAttempts: ⏳ Too many attempts. Please wait a minute and try again.
telegram.linkUsage:
Send the code right after the command: `/link CODE`

You'll find your connection code in Capka's settings.
telegram.invalidCode: 🤔 That code is invalid or has expired. Generate a new one in Capka and try again.
telegram.linked:
✅ **Account linked!**

Just message me and I'll get going. You can send text, photos and files.
telegram.linkFirst: Connect your account first. Open Capka and sign in with Telegram — it takes a few seconds and links automatically.
telegram.newChat:
🆕 **New chat**

Your previous conversation is saved. Go ahead — we're starting fresh.
telegram.notLinked:
To let me reply, connect your account first.

Open Capka and sign in with Telegram — it takes a few seconds and links automatically.
telegram.tooFast: ⏳ Hold on a moment — you're sending messages too fast.
telegram.budgetReached: 💳 Spending limit reached. Please contact your admin to raise it.
telegram.fileTooBig: 📎 `{name}` is larger than 20 MB, so Telegram won't let me fetch it.
telegram.unsupported: 🤔 For now I work with text, photos and files. Send me one of those and I'll get going.
telegram.startError:
⚠️ Couldn't start the assistant. Please try again in a moment.

`{error}`
telegram.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
telegram.pendingApproval:
⏳ **Account awaiting approval**

An administrator needs to approve your account. You'll be able to chat as soon as they do.
telegram.capabilityNotice: ⚠️ The current model can't process this attachment ({modalities}). Use /model to pick one that supports it.
telegram.modelPrompt: 🧠 Pick a model for this chat:
telegram.modelEmpty: No models available yet. Add a provider in Capka settings.
telegram.modelStale: That list is out of date — send /model again.
telegram.confirmExpired: This confirmation expired — just ask again.
telegram.confirmFailed: Couldn't apply that — please try again.

mcp.probeUnreachable: Couldn't reach it just now — you can still add it and fix later.
mcp.probeNeedsAuth: Reachable, but it needs a sign-in or token (set on the settings page).
mcp.probeOauth: You'll sign in through your browser after adding it.

skill.repoEmpty: No matching skills found in that repo.
skill.repoUnreachable: Couldn't read the repo just now — you can still install; it'll pull on confirm.
skill.repoInstalled: {n, plural, one {# skill} other {# skills}} from {repo}
skill.pathEmpty: No SKILL.md found at that path.
skill.pathUnreachable: Couldn't read that path just now — you can still install; it'll be read on confirm.
skill.pathInstalled: {n, plural, one {# skill} other {# skills}} from {path}
skill.editReady: "{name}" is checked out to {path}/SKILL.md — edit the files there, then save with skill add {path}.
